title: Garage Door IoT Controller
description: ESP32-based WiFi controller for a motorised garage door. Two reed switches detect fully-open and fully-closed positions; a solid-state relay triggers the door motor; PIR sensor detects presence; MQTT publishes door state; OTA firmware updates via HTTPS. Companion smartphone app via MQTT dashboard.
objective: Remote open/close via MQTT; door position confirmed via reed switches within 15 s; PIR presence alert on MQTT; OTA update without physical access; retain last state through power-cycles.
constraints: 230 V garage supply; ESP32 in IP54 enclosure; ≤ £30 BOM; no proprietary cloud; MQTT broker on local network; TLS OTA.
supporting_fact_titles: MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data
OTA Firmware Update for Embedded Devices
Magnetic Reed Switch for Contact/Position Detection
PIR Passive Infrared Motion Sensor — HC-SR501
design_element_titles: Magnetic Reed Switch Door Position Sensor

element_usage_notes.Magnetic Reed Switch Door Position Senso: N/O reed pairs at closed-limit (GPIO 34, pull-up) and open-limit (GPIO 35, pull-up); LOW = magnet present = position reached.
element_usage_notes.PIR HC-SR501 Motion Detection Module: HC-SR501 on GPIO 32; H-retriggering mode; sensitivity 7 m; publishes garage/door/presence on motion.
element_usage_notes.Solid-State Relay Mains Actuator Switchi: Fotek SSR-25DA on GPIO 25; 3.3 V compatible control input; 230 V/10 A output to door motor activation circuit; 300 ms pulse triggers door motor.
element_usage_notes.OTA HTTPS Dual-Bank Firmware Update: ArduinoOTA over local WiFi; triggered via MQTT command garage/door/cmd = 'ota'; secure: password-protected.
element_usage_notes.ESP32 WiFi + MQTT IoT Telemetry: WiFi STA mode; broker at 192.168.1.x:1883; topics: garage/door/state, garage/door/cmd, garage/door/presence. Retained state messages.
element_usage_notes.MQTT QoS-1 Secure Event Logger: All door transitions and presence events logged to garage/door/log with ISO 8601 timestamp.

element_interactions.from: Magnetic Reed Switch Door Position Sensor
element_interactions.data: GPIO level change (open-limit or closed-limit asserted LOW)
element_interactions.description: Interrupt-on-change on GPIO 34 and 35. State machine: OPEN if open-limit LOW; CLOSED if closed-limit LOW; MOVING if neither (timeout 15 s → publish 'stuck').
Rising-edge interrupt triggers MQTT publish to garage/door/presence = 'motion'. No action taken on door — presence is informational only.
MQTT subscription to garage/door/cmd = 'toggle' asserts GPIO 25 HIGH for 300 ms, simulating a momentary wall-button press on the door motor controller.
Every state transition (OPEN/CLOSED/MOVING/STUCK) and presence event published to garage/door/log with UTC timestamp.
On receiving 'ota' command, ESP32 fetches firmware binary from a pre-configured HTTPS URL, verifies SHA256 hash, writes to inactive partition, and reboots.
